source: Sun et al., GPTScan: Detecting Logic Vulnerabilities in Smart Contracts by Combining GPT with Program Analysis, ICSE 2024
methodology_note: Cited from GPTScan paper Tables. Self-host pipeline is feasible (see baselines/gptscan/INSTALL.md) but requires Java + multiple Python deps + 6-8 hours full sweep; cited path is faster. GPTScan is LLM-static (one-shot LLM call per function with rule library) — TTE = LLM call latency, typically 30-60s on gpt-3.5-turbo. Original paper used GPT-4; our self-host plan uses NVIDIA NIM gpt-oss-120b for cost.

completed_steps: pip install requirements (minus pysha3 / dbus-python / falcon-analyzer)
create sha3 shim using pycryptodome Keccak
patch chatgpt_api.py for NIM endpoint + model env vars (script: baselines/gptscan/patch_chatgpt_api.py)
remaining_steps: sudo apt install default-jre default-jdk python3.11-venv
create dedicated .venv to isolate openai 0.27.8 from main pipeline
